```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))

## Parâmetros aleatórios
n      <- sample(c(25, 36, 64, 100), 1)
lambda <- sample(c(2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8), 1)
delta  <- sample(-3:3, 1)
sum_x  <- n * lambda + delta

## Resposta correta
correct <- round(sum_x / n, 4)

## Alternativas incorretas plausíveis
w1 <- round(sum_x / n + 0.5, 4)
w2 <- round(sum_x * (n - 1) / n^2, 4)
w3 <- round(sum_x / n + 1 / n, 4)
w4 <- round(sum_x / n - 1 / n, 4)

## Garantir unicidade
if (w1 == correct) w1 <- w1 + 0.1
if (w2 == correct) w2 <- w2 + 0.2
if (w2 == w1)      w2 <- w2 - 0.15
if (w3 == correct || w3 == w1 || w3 == w2) w3 <- round(sum_x / (n + 1), 4)
if (w4 == correct || w4 == w1 || w4 == w2 || w4 == w3) w4 <- round(sum_x / n - 0.5, 4)

## Embaralhamento SEMPRE
all_vals <- c(correct, w1, w2, w3, w4)
# Garante cinco alternativas numericamente distintas após a parametrização.
integer_choices <- all(abs(all_vals - round(all_vals)) < sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))
choice_step <- if (integer_choices) 1 else max(1e-4, max(abs(all_vals), na.rm = TRUE) * 0.01)
while (anyDuplicated(all_vals)) {
  duplicate_position <- which(duplicated(all_vals))[1]
  all_vals[duplicate_position] <- max(all_vals, na.rm = TRUE) + choice_step
}
choices <- sample(all_vals)
sol_pos <- which(choices == correct)
sol_str <- paste(as.integer(seq_along(choices) == sol_pos), collapse = "")
```

Question
========

Seja $X_i \overset{\text{iid}}{\sim} \text{Poisson}(\lambda)$. Como $E(X) = \lambda$, o estimador pelo método dos momentos é $\hat{\lambda}_{MM} = \bar{X}_n$. Dado que $n = `r n`$ e a soma $\sum_{i=1}^{`r n`} X_i = `r sum_x`$, calcule $\hat{\lambda}_{MM}$.

Answerlist
----------

* `r choices[1]`
* `r choices[2]`
* `r choices[3]`
* `r choices[4]`
* `r choices[5]`

Solution
========

Para $X_i \overset{\text{iid}}{\sim} \text{Poisson}(\lambda)$, temos $E(X) = \lambda$. O método dos momentos iguala o primeiro momento teórico ao amostral: $\lambda = m_1 = \bar{X}_n$. Portanto:
$$\hat{\lambda}_{MM} = \bar{X}_n = \frac{\sum_{i=1}^n X_i}{n} = \frac{`r sum_x`}{`r n`} = `r correct`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap3S1Q2_emm_poisson
extype: schoice
exsolution: `r sol_str`
exshuffle: FALSE
